title: The quantum group structure of long-range integrable deformations
abstract: Quantum integrable spin chains are known to possess a large family of long-range deformations generated by the local, boost and bilocal operators. Although these deformations are well-understood on the level of the pairwise commuting charges, the underlying quantum group structures had not yet been recognised. In this paper, we provide a quantum group-theoretical description for the family of long-range deformations of arbitrary homogeneous Yang-Baxter integrable spin chains up to first order in the deformation parameter. In particular, we show that the deformations are obtained via a twist of the algebraic structure of the underlying quantum group. This twisting results in a generally non-associative algebra that has a non-trivial Drinfeld associator. The Drinfeld associator is then shown to encode the information about the long-range interaction terms for the integrable spin chain. Moreover, the deformed quantum group is shown to contain a large perturbatively associative substructure, thus ensuring the perturbative integrability of the long-range model. The deformed quantum group provides explicit expressions for the Lax operators and R-matrices of the long-range deformed models, which manifestly satisfy the RLL relation and the Yang-Baxter equation up to first order in the deformation parameter. In order to derive the results, we introduce algebra elements that we call the algebraic charge densities. As a side result, we provide a conjecture for the explicit expressions of the undeformed charge densities in terms of these algebra elements.
summary: This paper provides a quantum group-theoretical description of long-range integrable deformations in spin chains. It reveals that these deformations arise from a twisting process that introduces non-associativity, with the Drinfeld associator acting as the key mechanism for encoding long-range interactions.
main_problem: The paper aims to identify the underlying quantum group structure for long-range integrable deformations of spin chains, which had previously been unrecognized despite the known properties of their commuting charges.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that long-range deformations are obtained via a twist of the underlying quantum group, resulting in a non-associative algebra characterized by a non-trivial Drinfeld associator that encodes the long-range interaction terms.
method: The study employs an algebraic framework using FRT-bialgebras, the quantum inverse scattering method, and a perturbative approach up to the first order in the deformation parameter, utilizing tools like the Sutherland equation and Drinfeld associators.
model_or_system: The research focuses on arbitrary homogeneous Yang-Baxter integrable spin chains, including specific examples like the Heisenberg (XXX) and Haldane-Shastry spin chains, subject to long-range deformations generated by local, boost, and bilocal operators.
key_observables: Conserved charges (pairwise commuting charges), R-matrices, Lax operators, transfer matrices, and algebraic charge densities.
important_parameters: The deformation parameter (lambda) and the spectral parameters (u, v).
main_assumptions: The results are provided only up to the first order in the deformation parameter, and the R-matrix is assumed to be analytic around the origin.
paper_structure: The paper begins by establishing the FRT-bialgebra framework and its duality with the Drinfeld-Jimbo approach, then introduces the twisting procedure to describe long-range deformations, proves the vanishing of the Drinfeld associator to establish the quantum group structure, and concludes with explicit constructions of Lax operators and connections to the Theta-morphism.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
